title: GSAP Scroll Animation Spec
body: # GSAP Scroll Animation Spec ## Library Setup ```bash npm install gsap ``` Use GSAP core + ScrollTrigger plugin only. No extra plugins. Bundle impact: ~40KB gzip. ```ts // src/utils/animations.ts import { gsap } from 'gsap'; import { ScrollTrigger } from 'gsap/ScrollTrigger'; gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger); export { gsap, ScrollTrigger }; ``` **Reduced-motion guard** (required — most traffic is mobile): ```ts const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches; if (prefersReducedMotion) return; ``` ## Animation Inventory ### 1. Section Headlines — Fade Up | Selector | Animation | Duration | Easing | |----------|-----------|----------|---------| | `.section-headline` | y: 24→0, opacity: 0→1 | 0.6s | power2.out | | Trigger | top 85% of viewport | | | ### 2. Framework Cards — Staggered Fade In | Selector | Animation | Duration | Stagger | |----------|-----------|----------|---------| | `.framework-card` | y: 32→0, opacity: 0→1 | 0.5s | 0.10s | | Trigger | `.framework-cards-grid` top 80% | | | ### 3. How It Works Steps — Sequential Slide In | Selector | Animation | Duration | Stagger | |----------|-----------|----------|---------| | `.step` | x: -20→0, opacity: 0→1 | 0.5s | 0.15s | | Trigger | `.steps` top 75% | | | Left-slide reads as sequence/steps more naturally than fade-down. ### 4. Comparison Cards — Fade In from Below | Selector | Animation | Duration | Stagger | |----------|-----------|----------|---------| | `.comparison-card` | y: 40→0, opacity: 0→1 | 0.6s | 0.12s | | Trigger | `.comparison-grid` top 80% | | | ### 5. Stat / Number Count-Up (future-ready) Homepage doesn't currently have stat numbers. Spec is ready for when social-proof stats are added (e.g. "12,000 tests taken"). ```ts // Usage: 0 gsap.utils.toArray('[data-count]').forEach((el) => { const target = parseInt((el as HTMLElement).dataset.count || '0'); gsap.to(el as Element, { scrollTrigger: { trigger: el as Element, start: 'top 85%' }, innerHTML: target, duration: 1.5, ease: 'power1.out', snap: { innerHTML: 1 }, }); }); ``` ## Mobile Performance Rules 1. **No 3D transforms** (rotateX, perspective) — GPU overdraw on mobile 2. **Only animate opacity and transform** — compositor-layer, no layout reflow 3. **Stagger ≤ 0.15s** — longer stagger feels broken on small screens 4. **Max duration 0.6s** — longer feels sluggish on phone 5. **Always bail on prefers-reduced-motion** — accessibility and battery 6. **ScrollTrigger `once: true`** — don't re-trigger on reverse scroll ## Integration Point Register in `useEffect` in `HomePage.tsx` (or a custom `useScrollAnimations` hook). Clean up with `ScrollTrigger.getAll().forEach(t => t.kill())` on unmount. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 4 section animation types work on desktop and mobile Chrome - [ ] Zero animation when `prefers-reduced-motion` is set - [ ] Only `opacity` and `transform` properties animated - [ ] `ScrollTrigger` uses `once: true` - [ ] Lighthouse Performance score does not drop >5 points vs baseline
